title: India's exports to UK top $140 million on first day of trade pact rollout
content:
• India exported more than $140 million worth of goods to the UK on the first day of the implementation of the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA).
• India and the UK are targeting $100 billion in bilateral trade by 2030 under the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA), which was signed in July 2025.
• Under the India-UK CETA, Indian exporters will receive duty-free access for 99.5% of the value of India's exports to the UK, covering 98.8% of tariff lines.

title: Govt weighing IT Rules amendment for OTT platforms, action against ZEE5: Report
content:
• The government is considering amending the IT Rules 2021 for mandatory certification of films before their release on OTT platforms.
• Action is being considered against intermediary ZEE5 for screening the uncensored film 'Satluj', which was released on July 3 and taken down two days later following a government order citing national security concerns.
• Part III of the Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules, 2021, empowers the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting (MIB) to invoke Section 69A of the IT Act to block online content.

title: India's direct tax body notifies Cost Inflation Index for this fiscal year
content:
• The Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) has notified the Cost Inflation Index (CII) as 376 for the fiscal year 2026.
• The notified Cost Inflation Index (CII) will apply to the tax year 2026-27, effective from April 1, 2026, and subsequent tax years.
• The Cost Inflation Index (CII) is a measure defined under Section 48 of the Income Tax Act, 1961, with its base year being 2001-02, assigned a value of 100.

title: RBI pushes banks, NBFCs to own data risks across third parties
content:
• The RBI has sought public comments by August 17 on its draft proposals titled 'Guidance on Regulatory Expectations for Data Governance'.
• Under the RBI's proposed framework, regulated entities (banks, NBFCs) will remain accountable for all data shared with third parties.
• The RBI's draft guidance specifies that data sharing by regulated entities should not result in unauthorised reuse, sharing or duplication.

title: India, EU hopeful of investment protection pact before year-end
content:
• India and the EU are hopeful of concluding an Investment Protection Agreement before the end of the year, as expressed by Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal and EU Trade and Economic Security Commissioner Maros Sefcovic.
• The third meeting of the India-EU Trade and Technology Council (TTC) was held in Brussels.
• External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar identified the free trade agreement, security and defence partnership, and mobility framework as key achievements of the India-EU partnership this year.

title: Softer inflation trips rate hike forecasts, RBI hold more likely
content:
• The RBI’s rate-setting committee will meet from August 3 to August 5, and the policy rate stands at 5.25%, having remained unchanged in the last three policy reviews.
• The RBI has assumed an average crude oil price of $95 per barrel for FY27 and projects fullyear FY27 inflation at 5.1% (revised from 4.6% in April).
• Consumer inflation averaged 3.9% in April-June, which is lower than the RBI's forecast of 4.2% for the same period.

title: India-UK trade pact comes into force, gold standard of FTA for both
content:
• The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) and a social security agreement became operational from July 15.
• India and the UK aim to increase bilateral trade to $100 billion by 2030 from nearly $60 billion in FY26. The UK eliminates duties on 99% of Indian goods from Day One, and India offers zero tariff on 64% of UK exports, gradually rising to 85%.
• CETA provides Indian suppliers legal access to the UK government procurement market worth around £90 billion ($122 billion) and allows Indian exporters to ship over 11 lakh tonnes of steel annually to the UK duty-free.

title: India exports $140 million worth of goods at zero duty on first day of UK trade pact
content:
• The Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) and an Agreement on Social Security between India and the UK became operational from July 15, allowing nearly 99% of Indian exports to enter the UK at zero duty.
• India and the UK aim to increase bilateral trade to $100 billion by 2030 from the current level of close to $60 billion in FY26.
• The CETA provides Indian suppliers legal access to the UK government procurement market worth around £90 billion ($122 billion) and exempts Indian professionals on temporary UK assignments of up to 60 months from making social security contributions in both countries.

title: UK-India trade pact 'gold standard' and powerful message for rules-based order: UK envoy Lindy Cameron
content:
• The UK-India free trade agreement, officially known as the Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA), took effect on Wednesday, securing zero-duty market access for nearly 99% of India's exports to the UK.
• The deal is projected to increase India's and the UK's GDP by nearly five billion pounds and boost bilateral trade by over 25 billion pounds every year, with a target to double bilateral trade by 2030.
• From its effective date, import duty on Scotch whisky has been cut from 150% to 75%, and duties on premium UK-built cars will fall from over 110% to 10% under a new quota.

title: How to crack a 'great deal' with India? UK reveals details of dealing with 'phenomenal negotiators'
content:
• The UK expects bilateral trade with India to increase by over £25 billion annually following the trade pact.
• Bilateral trade between the UK and India is currently valued at £48 billion.
• The UK FTA aims to reduce tariffs, improve market access for businesses and professionals, and deepen cooperation in trade, investment, and services.
